title: General Aptitude - Quantitative Aptitude - Sequence Series

questions.question:
Consider a sequence of numbers \( \large (\epsilon _{n}: n= 1, 2,...) \), such that \( \epsilon _{1}=10 \) and

\( \large \epsilon _{n+1}=\dfrac{20\epsilon _{n}}{20+\epsilon _{n}} \)

for \( n\geq 1 \). Which of the following statements is true?

Hint: Consider the sequence of reciprocals.



Quantitative Aptitude: TIFR CSE 2013 | Part A | Question: 19
options: The sequence \( \large (\epsilon _{n}: n= 1, 2,...) \) is decreasing and converges to \( 1. \)
The sequence \( \large (\epsilon _{n}: n= 1, 2,...) \) is decreasing and then increasing. Finally it converges to \( 1. \)

questions.question:
Consider the sequence \( \langle s_n : n \geq 0 \rangle \) defined as follows: \( s_0=0, s_1=1, s_2=1 \), and \( s_n = s_{n-1} +s_{n-2}+s_{n-3} \), for \( n \geq 3 \). Which of the following statements is FALSE?



Quantitative Aptitude: TIFR CSE 2016 | Part A | Question: 10
options: \( s_{4k} \) is even, for any \( k \geq 0 \)
\( s_{4k+1} \) is odd, for any \( k \geq 0 \)
\( s_{4k+2} \) is odd, for any \( k \geq 0 \)
\( s_n \) is a multople of 3, for only finitely many values of \( n \)
\( s_{4k+3} \) is even, for any \( k \geq 0 \)
questions.correct_answer: \( s_n \) is a multople of 3, for only finitely many values of \( n \)

questions.question:
Let \( S \) be the value of following infinite series:
\[
\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n^{4}} .
\]
In which of the following intervals must \( S \) lie?



Quantitative Aptitude: TIFR CSE 2023 | Part A | Question: 9
options: The series diverges, so \( S \) must be infinity.
